storyInfo: Detective Koretou of the Mitsukue police targets Hoshiguma as his prime suspect in a twenty-year-old murder case, but the statute of limitations has already expired. Hoshiguma's return to Mitsukue has nothing to do with the case—it's because of a letter from Tessai, the head of the Kinsekikai yakuza clan, a man like a second father to her. The police are left scratching their heads, while Kouhei Mifune, another Kinsekikai yakuza, shows up at the police station and pulls Hoshiguma out.

attributes.content: Here's your fried meatchop and rice, detective.
attributes.name: Officer on Patrol

attributes.content: Good job. Go get some rest.
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: Thank you!
attributes.name: Officer on Patrol

attributes.content: It's past midnight already. You've been hiding from us ever since you got to Mitsukue this afternoon. You must be hungry, you haven't eaten at all.
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: (Nods)
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Alright then. Here, a fried meatchop with rice, Higashinese specialty.
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: The detective puts extra emphasis on the word Higashinese and pushes the tray toward Hoshiguma.
With nary a word, Hoshiguma picks up the bowl and chopsticks and begins shoveling rice down her gullet.
Well? How is it? Do they have food like this in Lungmen?
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: (Continues shoveling rice)
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Would you look at that. Tell me, just how long has it been since you've enjoyed a proper Higashinese meal?
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: (Shoves chop into mouth)
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Whatever... eat up.
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: (Noisily chews and crunches cabbage)
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: (Gulps water)
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: (Tilts head back and dumps remaining contents of bowl into mouth)
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: (Puts down empty bowl)
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Phew.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Tastes like home, doesn't it?
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: It's not a home or nostalgia thing, Detective-san. I'm just hungry.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Why you——
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: Actually, I had a fried meatchop with rice right before I left Lungmen. Next time, could you get me something else? Like some oden from the place you dragged me out of? It smelled amazing—I didn't even get a sip of the broth.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: And for the record, when I was footing the bill to feed suspects back in Lungmen, I always ordered cheongfun.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: I suggest you start taking this a bit more seriously, Hoshiguma. You're the suspect this time.
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: Me? I haven't even been in Mitsukue for twenty-four hours. What could you possibly suspect me of?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: A suspect in a case from twenty years ago isn't a suspect?
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: Detective-san...
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Detective Koretou.
attributes.name: Detective

attributes.content: Detective Koretou.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Yes?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: After twenty years, we're well past the statute of limitations.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Besides, you should be about done authenticating my Lungmen inspector's badge, no?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: We haven't gotten the results back yet.
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Let's assume you're telling me the truth. What would motivate a Lungmen cop like you to come all the way back here?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Did you lose your job? You have some enemies you need to deal with?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Did someone in Lungmen send you here?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Or are you perhaps connected to those rumors about the spy of the Northern Court?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Stop, stop, stop. If you keep throwing out guesses like that, this whole conversation is gonna spiral...
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Take a look at this letter, Detective.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: We just searched you—where the hell were you hiding that?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: It takes a detective to outsmart a detective.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Phew.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Let's run another lap. How about it, partner? Think you can keep up with me?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Looks like you're good. Let's go.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: (Is that the lady's car?)
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: The single-horned oni lays across the vehicle, sizes up the end of the straightaway, then charges dead ahead.
The howling winds make it hard for her to tell just how far her pursuers are.
What brings you here, ma'am?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: I'm just stopping by. I don't plan on staying long.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: Yenwu told me that before Swire set out for New Siesta, she tidied up all internal affairs, to lighten the load for those of you left behind.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: Yeah. If Missy hadn't wrapped things up before leaving, how could I have found the time to come here?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: That's true. Then what is it that's troubling you, Inspector Hoshiguma?
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: You can see it in my face, huh?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: I wouldn't say so.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: However, if repeatedly pushing the pedal to the metal still leaves you wrestling with anxiety, you shouldn't bottle all that emotion up on your own.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: You and I are both Higashinese. When Little Ch'en and Swire aren't here, I have to act in their stead.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: We are... aren't we?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Indeed we are. Higashinese.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: I can't hide anything from you, ma'am. I recently received a letter...
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.text: Summer's end is nigh, yet the stubborn heat still lingers—please mind your good health. Twenty years have passed with nary a word between us. Much has transpired—things best left unwritten. Yet I feel compelled to write to you, though I regret any trouble this may cause.
\nThis letter is written for no other reason than the decade or so that we spent together in our hometown of Kajimachi, which is soon to vanish from the map.
\nThough the land is unaware, its people still have feelings; therefore, I have humbly taken it upon myself to organize the funeral rites for Kajimachi, which will be held soon.
\nIn my waning years, to see old friends once more would be a grand blessing.

attributes.content: What a solemn, I mean, traditional letter.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: This is the man who arranged for your refuge in Lungmen—the so-called 'father' of Hannya?
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: That's right. I called him Uncle Tessai. It's been so many years, he must be in his seventies or eighties by now.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: I lived in Kajimachi before I left Mitsukue.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: And so, is it not only proper to return to Kajimachi one last time, before it is no more?
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: To be honest, when I first came to Lungmen, I considered settling here for good. I thought about moving elsewhere if things didn't work out, even leaving Yan altogether, the same way I left Higashi—but the one thing I never considered was going back home.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: It's been twenty years. I don't even know what Mitsukue is like these days. Instead of going back home, I might as well come here for a ride and to grab a plate of street food—stir-fried noodles—to put my mind at ease.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Don't you have a good reason to go home? The funeral, no?
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: You've volunteered to serve as a mighty guardian of Lungmen, throwing yourself into the fight without hesitation. Yenwu, myself, Lin, and all your friends—we've seen it with our own eyes.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: But you shouldn't limit yourself. You're more than that.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: Little Ch'en is gone, and Yühsia has more or less taken over for her father. One L.G.D. Commissioner alone won't keep this operation afloat.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: I have neglected my responsibilities.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Nonsense.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: Before Little Ch'en left Lungmen, you had words with her. They eventually reached my ears as well, whether I wanted to hear them or not.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: Even if the problems you've bottled up inside can never be resolved, simply going back to have a chat with him while he still walks among the living is worth your while, yes?
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: You're right.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: By the way, don't forget to bring back a few packs of proper Higashinese senbei for me?
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: Senbei?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: A 'jianbing' shop opened up near the L.G.D. I thought it was Higashinese senbei, with the name translated, so I went and placed an order.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: It wasn't until the piping-hot pancake came out—stuffed with fowlbeast egg, crispy meat, and scallions—that I realized it wasn't a senbei at all, but a Jiangqi jianbing. These are completely different dishes.
attributes.name: Fumizuki

attributes.content: I think we've resolved any and all misunderstandings between us, right Detective Koretou?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: The oyabun of the Kinsekikai yakuza clan sent you a letter, inviting you to the funeral in Kajimachi, his turf. And after some persuading from a particular individual of similar origin, you've decided to go back there—am I right?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: That's right.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: This particular individual...
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Her identity is not important.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Is it not? If it was not for her, would you dare walk the streets of Mitsukue?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Of course I would!
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Alright. If it really isn't important, then you can stay here a good twenty-four hours.
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: The station's quiet these days anyway. Hardly any of the calls we get are real emergencies.
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Then what are they about?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: They're reporting signs of a kaidan they want me to investigate.
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: A kaidan...? Like an urban legend? Ghost story?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Ha. You really haven't been back to Mitsukue for a while, huh?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: This place doesn't even feel like a real police station any more—the townies have been treating us like cops from the Spiritual Affairs Station for a while now...
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Who is it?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: There's someone outside for you... it's...Mifune-san.
attributes.name: Officer on Patrol

attributes.content: The weary-looking detective glances at Hoshiguma, then out the door; hatred is seared across his face.
If you're in the middle of something here, do you want me to stall him for a bit?
attributes.name: Officer on Patrol

attributes.content: You? Stall him? You won't even get three words in before he calls someone else—and then that call will be routed straight to us.
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Send him in——
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Before the detective can finish speaking, a tall Phidia strides into the room. Ignoring the officers around him, he makes a beeline for Hoshiguma.
Kinbee?!
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Never fear, Hoshiguma. As long as I'm here, the police can't touch you.
attributes.name: Kinbee?

attributes.content: (Laughs dryly) Ha, ha. Kouhei Mifune-san—been a while since someone called you by your old name, from before you made it big, huh?
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Well, why don't you try it then?
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Good. Knowing when to yield is a virtue.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Mifune——
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Hoshiguma, you will always address me as Kinbee.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Come. Let's go home.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Detective Koretou still hasn't——
attributes.name: Officer on Patrol

attributes.content: Still hasn't what? Hasn't explained the basis for this arbitrary detention? Don't tell me this is about that case from twenty years ago?
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: What a pity—the statute of limitations already expired, many years ago actually. The Northern Court may have revised its penal code, but that doesn't give you southerners the right to bend the law.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Spare me the mudslinging. Even if the statute of limitations is up——
attributes.name: Officer on Patrol

attributes.content: Yes, the statute of limitations is up, so on what grounds are the police still detaining people? I want a justification—one that can withstand a Diet inquiry, a televised hearing, and the court of public opinion.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: This is intimidation!
attributes.name: Officer on Patrol

attributes.content: This is me exercising my rights under the law!
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Detective Koretou!
attributes.name: Officer on Patrol

attributes.content: ...Let them go.
attributes.name: Detective Koretou

attributes.content: Kinbee, Uncle Tessai sent you to get me?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: The entire Kinsekikai is awaiting your return. Let's go. Stop arguing with the cops.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: They exit the police station, turn right, and cross the street.
Nita Street unfolds before Hoshiguma, in broad daylight.
The harsh white sun shines down on the street, draining the buildings of their neon shimmer and baring the raw concrete beneath.

The morning commute is in full swing. Expressionless office workers hurry past shops that are either still closed or trying half-heartedly to drum up business—none of them look nearly as glamorous as they did under the moonlight.
Who was it that threw up on the street last night? Which man was persuaded to empty his wallet at the club? Under the light of day, they all look the same.
How many years has it been?! How're things in Lungmen? Still good? We hadn't heard a peep from you all this time. I was afraid something had happened to you. I was worried sick!
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: I figured, after all this time, nobody thought of me as one of the Kinsekikai anymore.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: How? No matter how fate plays its tricks or how long you've been incommunicado—as long as you come back, then you're still family.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Over the past two decades, has the Kinsekikai ever issued you an 'expulsion' or 'no-contact' notice? Never.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Say what you will, but even now, at every official meeting, the seat to the Old Man's left is still left empty. You've always been the Kinsekikai's wakagashira—the heir apparent.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: And you...?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Why, I'm just the wakagashira-hosa, the wakagashira's second.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Don't for a minute think the Old Man's gone senile. Everyone agrees with his decision.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: If you hadn't offed that Diet candidate twenty years ago, before I could get to him too, the Kinsekikai would've been driven out of Kajimachi—crushed under the weight of his public pressure campaign.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Then, you made sure the rest of us were in the clear, taking all the heat for the killing. You fled to Lungmen alone, which allowed the Kinsekikai to survive.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: You've sacrificed so much for us. If we just turned our backs on you, what kind of family would that make us?
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Don't say such things.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: It's all facts, clear as day, so why can't I state the facts?
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Kinbee, we can talk ancient history later.
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: Uncle Tessai mentioned in his letter that he was holding a funeral for Kajimachi. Do you know about this?
attributes.name: Hoshiguma

attributes.content: A what?!
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: A funeral...? No... What are we talking about with a funeral?
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: Madame Oni, we really don't have time to wander the streets right now. We need to get back to Kajimachi and find him as quick as we can to straighten all this out.
attributes.name: Mifune

attributes.content: The tall Phidia hastens his steps and hurries off towards Kajimachi.
He walks so fast, and with such long strides, that Hoshiguma has to break into a jog just to keep up.
